texto: I've been lucky. I've made films that I really like. It's been a combination of what comes to me and what I choose. I've gone after lots of things that I didn't get, pet projects that everybody ends up chasing after. Really, you're lucky if you get anything.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hope Davis: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “I've been lucky. I've made films that I really like. It's been a combination of what comes to me …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, me, lucky pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e lucky.

frase_original: I've been lucky. I've made films that I really like. It's been a combination of what comes to me and what I choose. I've gone after lots of things that I didn't get, pet projects that everybody ends up chasing after. Really, you're lucky if you get anything.

texto: You know what I have noticed? And this is really sad. Flying first class is less scary than flying coach. They speak to you and they're so nice to you and they want to help you and they know you want a drink before the plane takes off. And they bring it to you without asking. If you're sitting in coach and hoping for a drink, good luck.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Hope Davis que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “You know what I have noticed? And this is really sad. Flying first class is less scary than flyin…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, good, luck, com foco em luck e speak.

frase_original: You know what I have noticed? And this is really sad. Flying first class is less scary than flying coach. They speak to you and they're so nice to you and they want to help you and they know you want a drink before the plane takes off. And they bring it to you without asking. If you're sitting in coach and hoping for a drink, good luck.

texto: Every summer my husband and I pack our suitcases, load our kids into the car, and drive from tense, crowded New York City to my family's cottage in Maine. It's on an island, with stretches of sea and sandy beaches, rocky coasts, and pine trees. We barbecue, swim, lie around, and try to do nothing.
explicacao: Citação de Hope Davis sobre temas ligados a familia. “Every summer my husband and I pack our suitcases, load our kids into the car, and drive from tens…” articula familia, car, sea em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como sea e trees.
